title: papaver rhoeas f. bipinnatum
intro: Papaver rhoeas f. bipinnatum, also known as the pink poppy or corn poppy, is a variety of the common poppy plant that is characterized by its delicate pink flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce sweating, treat respiratory issues, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas f. bipinnatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve sleep, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
